title: 求有讀critical thinking嘅人幫幫手

replies.msg:
Identify and explain any linguistic pitfalls of the following passage: “Let me teach you an easy way for travelling in the space. Sit comfortably in the sofa and look through your window. You are now travelling more than 25,000 km a day since the Earth is revolving around the sun!”

個passage係有啲咩問題求解答
You are now travelling more than 25,000 km a day since the Earth is revolving around the sun!”

橋就我諗，
credit就你袋?
屌你老母

Sit comfortably in the sofa and look through your window. You are now travelling more than 25,000 km a day since the Earth is revolving around the sun

Comfortable>how come?
25,000>sources?
Revolving around the sun??

quote.msg:
Identify and explain any linguistic pitfalls of the following passage: “Let me teach you an easy way for travelling in the space. Sit comfortably in the sofa and look through your window. You are now travelling more than 25,000 km a day since the Earth is revolving around the sun!”

個passage係有啲咩問題求解答
應該係任何時候都travel緊？而唔需要sit in sofa, 所以做完呢樣野先you are now travelling係唔成立

replies.msg: 好 thankyou！！
Resolving around the sun at what speed?

travelling in space主語應該係you，唔係個earth
所以travelling in space = you離開個地球去太空旅行，而唔係個地球圍住太陽轉
坐係梳發上面係on Earth，唔係in space
利申無讀過，9up

quote.msg:
橋就我諗，
credit就你袋?
屌你老母

replies.msg: 做左好耐諗唔到先嚟問下

我無讀過你呢類型既科目
但好似幾有趣又9 up下先 (有錯/文法唔正確勿屌，利申文盲)
我唔懷疑佢內文所講既科學知識係錯
畢竟佢要搵既係"linguistic pitfalls" 唔係"scientific pitfalls"

The phrase "travelling in the space" is generally understood as travelling in the "outer space", which is the void located in-between the Earth's atmosphere and any other celestial objects in the milky way.

Based on the circumstance mentioned in the passage, "I" am on Earth and bounded by the gravity of Earth. Therefore, "I" am not travelling in the space. A more accurate way to describe "my" motion would be that "I am travelling in the milky way". (Who isn't?)
邊個reference frame 先
